sections.paragraph:
παρεῖπον
Etym. aor2, with no pres. in use, παρά-φημι or παρ-αγορεύω being used instead

to persuade by indirect means, to talk over, win over, Il., Aesch.; παρειπών by thy persuasions, Il.:—c. acc. cogn. to give such and such advice, αἴσιμα παρειπών Il.


Etym. In Il. the first syll. is long, πᾱρειπών, πᾱρειποῦσα, the orig. form having been παρϜειπών.
